title: pteridium falcatum
intro: Pteridium falcatum, also known as bracken, is a type of flowering plant in the fern family that is native to Europe, Asia, and North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. Bracken has been used to treat various health issues, such as arthritis, gout, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and improving gut health. Bracken can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, tincture, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves in hot water or a solvent.
